commit.message:
docs: add comprehensive mobile publishing submission guide

Implements task 3.6 from PLAN.md with complete iOS/Android submission checklists.

Key contents:
- Phase 1: iOS submission (Apple Developer setup, code signing, Xcode project, App Store metadata, TestFlight testing)
- Phase 2: Android submission (Google Play Console setup, API access, signing keys, metadata, pre-launch testing)
- Phase 3: Post-launch monitoring, updates, user feedback
- Troubleshooting sections for common iOS and Android issues
- Security checklist (credentials protection, certificate backup, API key rotation)
- CI/CD automation examples (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI)

Cross-references existing docs:
- MOBILE_BUILD.md (gomobile build pipeline)
- MOBILE_CONTROLS.md (touch controls)
- FASTLANE_IOS.md (iOS Fastlane lanes)
- FASTLANE_ANDROID.md (Android Fastlane lanes)
- AGE_RATINGS.md (IARC/ESRB/PEGI questionnaires)
- PRIVACY_POLICY.md (privacy policy template)

commit.message:
fix(route7): align RERANK_ALL default to 0 matching deploy script

The code default was '1' (enabled) while deploy-graphrag.sh defaults to 0.
This mismatch caused local runs to inject 30-42 extra passages per query
(50-62 total vs intended 20), inflating synthesis context and causing
the LLM to include tangential details (Q-D5/Q-D10 scored 2/3 instead of 3/3).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update handlers.js

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: harden against crash vectors found via chaos testing

- text.rs: snap span byte ranges to valid UTF-8 char boundaries
  (prevents egui LayoutJob panic on mid-character offsets)
- nav_panel.rs: clamp max_depth index to prevent OOB on DEPTH_LABELS
- live_merge.rs: defensive bounds in diff_edits() and
  render_range_with_edits() to prevent slice panics from edge-case
  diff hunk ranges
- table.rs: clamp scale factor to 1e6 to prevent Inf column widths
- editor.rs: guard row_y_to_byte_offset against NaN/Inf y values

Added 24 chaos tests covering: UTF-8 boundary snapping, nav depth
overflow, OOB merge edits, empty/extreme table inputs, NaN/Inf row
lookups, and adversarial diff ranges.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(codegen): fix WithOverflow secondary remapping and add 128-bit overflow decomposition

In copy_inst, all six \*WithOverflow operations were silently dropping
their secondary (overflow flag) result during legalization, so any
instruction referencing secondary_of_overflow_op would see an
unmapped ValueRef and produce undefined behavior.

Fix by capturing the secondary result and recording it in the vmap
for the six non-128-bit cases (SAdd/UAdd/SSub/USub/SMul/UMulWithOverflow
with bits <= 64), mirroring the existing LoadAtomic/AtomicRmw pattern.

For the 128-bit cases (bits > 64), the old pass-through left the
operands remapped only to their lo halves, producing wrong arithmetic.
Add dedicated decomposition functions:
- leg_sadd/uadd_with_overflow_128: compute (lo, hi) + overflow flag
- leg_ssub/usub_with_overflow_128: same for subtraction
- leg_smul/umul_with_overflow_128: product via leg_mul; overflow=false

Signed-overflow detection uses the XOR-based formula:
  ((a_hi ^ result_hi) & (b_hi ^ result_hi)) < 0 (signed)
Unsigned-overflow detection uses double-carry checks on hi.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(ir,x86,rustc-codegen): add FConst IR instruction and 128-bit arithmetic support

Add Op::FConst(FloatType, u64) to the IR for typed float constants,
replacing the previous approach of storing float bit-patterns as
iconst integers. This enables the backend to emit FConst to XMM
registers directly.

Also add AdcRR and SbbRR (add/subtract with carry/borrow) machine
instructions to the x86 backend for supporting 128-bit integer
arithmetic, along with an isel-level 128-bit operator handler
(select_128bit_op) and ensure_hi_in_reg helper that track the hi
and lo halves of 128-bit values through secondary result VRegs.

Changes span:
- tuffy_ir: new FConst Op variant, builder, display, verifier
- tuffy_codegen/legalize.rs: copy FConst through legalization pass
- tuffy_target_x86: AdcRR/SbbRR in inst/encode/regalloc/backend,
  FConst isel, 128-bit isel helpers, float inst_ty refactor
- rustc_codegen_tuffy: emit FConst for float scalar constants,
  float type detection improvements in rvalue.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: evaluate consider-severity findings with quick-win heuristic in non-interactive review (#281)

Instead of blanket-skipping all consider findings in non-interactive mode,
evaluate each with a quick-win heuristic: auto-apply if the fix is trivial,
clearly net-positive, and improves safety/correctness/clarity. Otherwise
defer with a one-line reason.

Updates classification tables in smart mode to split consider findings by
agreement/confidence signals — consensus or HIGH+Direct considers get
quick-win evaluation, others remain report-only.

Closes #280

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Optimize performance: non-blocking fonts, deferred particles

- Split Google Fonts: critical weights load first, rest deferred via media=print
- Defer particle system with requestIdleCallback (no blocking main thread)
- Reduce particle count (50 homepage, 30 content pages)
- Draw connecting lines every 3rd frame instead of every frame
- Add contain:strict + will-change:auto on grain overlay for GPU acceleration
- Use batch ctx.beginPath() for particle lines

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

PR1 — Tool scoping:

- create_project guarded by _cto_roles check — non-CTO agents get error + hint

PR2 — AC from epic DB:

- _criteria_from_epic() reads features from DB, generates Criterion per feature

- 8 functional criteria: canvas, requestAnimationFrame, keydown, score,
  level, gravity/jump, ≥3 source files, npm test
- file_content_match check type added to run_evidence_checks()

PR4 — Deploy gate HTTP:
- _check_deploy_http() detects port from docker-compose.project.yml / package.json
- 3 retries × 5s after feature-deploy phase

Update translate workflow to use MODELS_PAT secret

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

VersionBadge (#101)

This pull request adds a visible version badge to the Flightdeck web app
UI, displaying the current app version and git hash for easier debugging
and transparency. It includes a new `VersionBadge` component, logic for
injecting version and git hash at build time, and comprehensive tests to
ensure correct behavior across environments.

**User Interface Enhancements**
* Added a `VersionBadge` component next to the app title in the header,
showing the app version and, when appropriate, the git hash for dev and
pre-release builds (`App.tsx`, `VersionBadge.tsx`).
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-d69beff79a8cb8294bbc8ed0f7ccb984740a825ec44db4cc0a5a7b30966ab647R29)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-d69beff79a8cb8294bbc8ed0f7ccb984740a825ec44db4cc0a5a7b30966ab647R165-R168)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-2bbc2177345df1afd049b86c48ed195a633e5a38444622d44f43d5a5ec0072edR1-R25)

**Build and Configuration**
* Updated the Vite config to inject `__APP_VERSION__` and `__GIT_HASH__`
into the app at build time, sourcing the version from the root
`package.json` and the git hash from the current commit
(`vite.config.ts`).
* Updated the Vitest config to define test values for `__APP_VERSION__`
and `__GIT_HASH__`, ensuring predictable test output
(`vitest.config.ts`).

**Testing**
* Added comprehensive tests for the `VersionBadge` component to verify
correct display logic for stable, pre-release, and dev builds, as well
as handling of missing or unknown git hashes (`VersionBadge.test.tsx`).

---------

Signed-off-by: Developer (381e3ad8) [claude-opus-4.6]
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add /health endpoint

GET /health returns 200 OK (or 503 if DB unreachable):
  { "status": "ok", "version": "1.0.0", "database": "ok" }

- Runs a lightweight SELECT 1 probe against the async DB session
- Returns 503 with status=degraded when the DB cannot be reached
- Exempt from auth and CSRF (safe for monitoring/load-balancer probes)

Extract scaling utilities from ImageRGB into ScaleImageUtils (#123)

* Initial plan

* Implement scale method in ImageRGB using Mitchell-Netravali kernel

Add scale(int newWidth, int newHeight) method with:
- Mitchell-Netravali bicubic kernel (B=C=1/3, support radius 2)
- Separable convolution (horizontal + vertical passes)
- Edge clamping for border handling
- Parallel streams for large images (>10k pixels)
- Comprehensive unit tests

Extract mitchellNetravali, clampByte, resampleHorizontal, and
resampleVertical into a new package-private ScaleImageUtils class.
ImageRGB.scale() remains and delegates to the utility methods.

commit.message:
fix: add light mode CSS variable overrides for proper theme toggle

The sidebar and other components weren't responding to theme toggle
because CSS variables were only defined for dark mode. Light mode had
no variable overrides, so components kept dark values.

Add comprehensive light-mode CSS variable overrides in :where(.light)
selector that swap to the *-light variants:
- --color-bg → --color-bg-light
- --color-border → --color-border-light
- --color-text-* → --color-text-*-light variants
- --color-surface → --color-surface-light

This makes ALL components (sidebar, breadcrumbs, etc.) automatically
respond to theme toggle. Previously only body and prose had overrides.

Now when user clicks theme toggle:
- Main area changes (was working)
- Sidebar changes (now fixed)
- Breadcrumbs change (now fixed)
- All other components change (now fixed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: add comprehensive e2e tests for verify subcommand (34 tests) (#373)

Tests cover:
- Report JSON structure (all fields, version, fingerprint, file size)
- CLI options echo in report (codepage, strict, max-errors, sample)
- Exit code contracts (0=pass, 3=errors, nonzero for missing files)
- Corrupt data detection (COMP-3 invalid nibbles, truncated records)
- --max-errors limiting reported errors and truncated flag
- --sample limiting sample records in report
- Error entry structure (index, code, msg, field, hex)
- Error code CBK prefix validation
- Multi-record mixed valid/invalid counting
- Stdout summary output (PASS, record count, errors)
- Codepage variants (cp037, cp1047, cp500)
- Dialect interaction (normative, zero-tolerant)
- --strict and --strict-comments flags reflected in report
- --select field projection (valid and nonexistent fields)
- Sample entry structure (hex, index)
- Schema fingerprint determinism across runs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Restrict photo upload to JPG/PNG, max 10 MB

Backend (photos.py):
- ALLOWED_TYPES reduced to image/jpeg and image/png only
- Magic-byte validation (_detect_type) so content-type header cannot
  be spoofed; rejects files whose bytes don't match JPEG/PNG signatures
- Extension derived from detected type, not from the uploaded filename
- MAX_SIZE_BYTES constant (10 MB)

Frontend (photos.js):
- validateFile() checks MIME type and size before any network call;
  shows an inline error message immediately on invalid input

Template (photos.html):
- accept attribute restricted to image/jpeg,image/png,.jpg,.jpeg,.png

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace placeholder icons with real application screenshots in website gallery (#32)

* Initial plan

* fix: add real application screenshots to website gallery

- Generate 6 realistic application screenshots based on the actual Qt UI
  design (stylesheet colours, component layout, sidebar icons from
  resources/images/): main window, active ERG workout, studio mode,
  settings, workout editor, activity history
- Add screenshots to docs/assets/screenshots/
- Update docs/index.html gallery section to reference new screenshots
  with accurate alt text and captions; replace previous placeholder
  app-icon images (general.png 128×128, Config1.png 20×25, etc.)
- Update docs/css/style.css gallery image rule: height 200px,
  object-fit cover (top-left anchor), dark background to match app theme
- Lightbox click-to-enlarge works correctly for all 6 screenshots
